original_abstract: Background: The theoretical, but especially the practical values of identifying the biochemical compounds from the Rosa canina L. fruits are of present interest, this aspect being illustrated by the numerous researches. It was reported that the Rosa canina L. fruit, with its high ascorbic acid, phenolics and flavonoids contents, have antioxidant, antimutagenic and anticarcinogenic effects. This study was performed on order to evaluate the amount of the main phytochemicals (vitamin C, total polyphenols, and total flavonoids) content and their antioxidant activity.
humanized_abstract: The Rosa canina L. fruits represent an interesting object from the point of view of both their theoretical and practical significance. Several research studies aim to evaluate their chemical composition by identifying the main biochemical constituents. The Rosa canina L. fruit being rich in ascorbic acid, phenolic and flavonoids, they show strong antioxidant, antimutagenic and anticarcinogenic activity. Therefore, the main purpose of our study is to assess the quantity of vitamin C, total polyphenols and total flavonoids as well as the antioxidant capacity of these fruit plants.

Therefore, the main purpose of our study is to assess the quantity of vitamin C, total polyphenols and total flavonoids as well as the antioxidant capacity of these fruit plants.
